items.title: A GPU model stack
items.scenario: You need PyTorch with a CUDA build that matches your GPU drivers.
items.explanation: conda install pulls a prebuilt PyTorch + CUDA combination, avoiding manual driver/toolkit setup.

items.title: Sharing a reproducible ML env
items.scenario: A research repo must run identically on a colleague's machine.
items.explanation: environment.yml plus conda env create recreates the same Python and binary dependencies.

items.title: Mixing conda and pip
items.scenario: Heavy compiled libs come from conda, but a Gen AI SDK is only on PyPI.
items.explanation: Install binaries via conda, then list pip packages under a pip: block in environment.yml.

items.title: Multiple Python versions
items.scenario: One project needs Python 3.10 and another 3.12.
items.explanation: conda create -n env python=3.10 lets each environment pin its own interpreter version.
